# terrible_vmware_vmware_folder (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against

### Optional

- `absolute_path` (String) The absolute path of the folder to create. The absolute path should include the datacenter and the folder type. The leading slash is not required. For example the absolute path could be /DC-01/vm/my/folder or DC-01/vm/my/folder One of `relative_path` or `absolute_path` must be specified.
- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `datacenter` (String) The name of the datacenter where the folder should be created. Only used if the `relative_path` option is used.
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of name→value).
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `folder_type` (String) The type of folder that should be created. The folder type controls what resources can be held in the folder, as well as the path where the folder is created. For example, a folder at path /DC-01/vm/my/folder has folder type 'vm'. Only used if the `relative_path` option is used.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `relative_path` (String) The relative path of the folder to create. The relative path should include neither the datacenter nor the folder type. For example the relative path for the folder /DC-01/vm/my/folder is my/folder One of `relative_path` or `absolute_path` must be specified.
- `remove_vm_data` (Boolean) Only used if state is `absent` If true, any VMs in the folder tree will be completely removed. This includes any disks or data associated with the VM. If false, any VMs in the folder tree are deregistered. This means they are removed from the vSphere inventory but their data is not deleted.
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `state` (String) Create `present` or remove `absent` a vSphere folder. When state is `present`, all folders in the specified path will be created if they are missing. When state is `absent`, all content inside the folder will be deleted. See `remove_vm_data`
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution

### Read-Only

- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `folder` (String) Identifying information about the folder
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
